caption:
Napa Soup Dumpling Lasagna

470 Calories
63gP | 10gC | 19gF

Ingredients:
150g Extra Lean Beef (Raw weight)
150g Shrimp (Raw weight)
Chopped Green Onion
1 Tsp Grated Ginger
½ Tbsp Minced Garlic
1 Tbsp Soy Sauce
½ Tbsp Sesame Oil
½ Tbsp Oyster Sauce
1 Tsp Brown Splenda
½  Tsp Salt
1 Tsp White Pepper
¼ Cup Chicken Broth
Napa Cabbage
Sesame Seeds

Sesame Oil Soy Sauce:
2 Tbsp of soy sauce
½ Tsp of sesame oil
Pinch of chili flakes/gochugaru

Instructions:
1. Into a bowl combine the following ingredients: 150g Extra Lean Beef (Raw weight), 150g Shrimp (Raw weight), Chopped Green Onion, 1 Tsp Grated Ginger, ½ Tbsp Garlic, 1 Tbsp Soy Sauce, ½ Tbsp Sesame Oil, ½ Tbsp Oyster Sauce, 1 Tsp Brown Splenda, ½ Tsp Salt, 1 Tsp White Pepper and mix.
2. Into an oven safe dish, line the bottom with napa cabbage then place about ½ of the filling mixture and flatten out. Place napa cabbage until completely covered. Repeat with a layer of filling and top with another layer of napa cabbage.
3.  Add about ¼ cup of chicken stock and steam until the mixture is cooked through (took around 10-12mins).
4. Top with green onions, sesame seeds, soy sauce mixture and enjoy!

Follow for daily healthy recipes!
author: Chris Lee
transcript: Ditch the wonton wrappers if you're trying to lose weight because you need to try this Napa Soup Dumpling Lasagna. It packs 63 grams of protein for only 470 calories. It has only 10 grams of carbs and packs so much flavor for something so simple and easy to make. Full recipes in the description. And if you like recipes like these, follow me for daily healthy recipes. Enjoy!
title: Napa Soup Dumpling Lasagna Recipe
one_liner: A low-carb, high-protein lasagna using napa cabbage instead of wonton wrappers, with beef and shrimp filling.
key_points: 63g protein, only 10g carbs, and 470 calories per serving
Replaces wonton wrappers with layered napa cabbage for a low-carb twist
Filling combines lean beef, shrimp, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and oyster sauce
Steamed in an oven-safe dish for 10-12 minutes and topped with sesame soy sauce

instructions: 1. Combine beef, shrimp, green onion, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, sesame oil, oyster sauce, brown Splenda, salt, and white pepper in a bowl and mix well.
2. Line the bottom of an oven-safe dish with napa cabbage, add half the filling and flatten. Cover with napa cabbage, repeat with remaining filling, and top with a final layer of napa cabbage.
3. Add ¼ cup chicken broth and steam until cooked through, about 10-12 minutes.
4. Mix 2 tbsp soy sauce, ½ tsp sesame oil, and a pinch of chili flakes for the sauce. Top dish with green onions, sesame seeds, and the sauce before serving.
